title: 77L Glass Measuring Cup, [Insulated handle, V-Shaped Spout], High Borosilicate Glass Measuring Cup for Kitchen or Restaurant, Easy To Read, 500 ML (0.5 Liter, 2 Cup)
description:
About this item
Improved V-Shaped Spout: The V-shaped spout allows for better flow control, preventing spills and ensuring accurate measurements every time
Acid and Alkali-Resistant Measurement Scales: The acid and alkali-resistant measurement scales in three units (Cup, ML, OZ) ensure that you can measuring all types of liquids and follow any cookbook
High Borosilicate Glass: Glass measuring cup is made from high-quality borosilicate glass, making it resistant to sudden temperature changes from -4°F to 302°F. This means that it can be used safely on gas stoves, microwaves, ceramic stoves, ovens, and refrigerators. The insulated handle also ensures that your hands are protected from the heat
Comfortable and Lightweight Design: The high borosilicate glass measuring cup is lightweight, making it easy to handle and reducing the strain on your wrists. This makes it excellent for use in the kitchen, whether you're cooking, making coffee, or baking
Easy to Clean: The measuring cup has a wide mouth design, making it easy to clean by hand. It can also be safely washed in the dishwasher, but be aware that over time, this may affect the clarity of the markings
reviews_5s:
I usually drink 2 cups of coffee in the AM, but could never find a mug big enough to accommodate 2 cups, so I always had to brew two separate cups. I don't use an electric coffee maker anymore because of possible exposure to toxic metal & plastic/rubber materials used in the coffee maker. Instead, I use a simple "clean" and non-toxic Hario ceramic coffee dripper (Japanese made) along with natural (brown) paper filters. With this mug I now only have to boil water and brew once. This "measuring cup" is also of a much cleaner borosilicate glass. The pour lip works perfect and does not overflow like most measuring cups. I've tested with repeated use now and this cup stands up to warming on a somewhat hot coffee mug warmer and has not failed. Regardless of whether you use it solely for its intended use as a measuring cup, it certainly does work super for a big huge Cup O' Joe in the morning...!A measuring pitcher that is is so elegant I leave it sitting out on my kitchen counter and use it almost exclusively throughout the day. The white measurement markings are large and easy to read. The pitcher feels very light and delicate yet is proving to be very sturdy so far. I have used it for several mornings now with my immersion blender not only scrambling eggs but also preparing my butter coffee. I thought this was dishwasher safe but it is not. On my third time through dishwasher all the measurement markings washed away 😓. The packaging was also wonderful the pitcher was perfectly surrounded by foam in the box. And the exterior box packed by Amazon was very well done as well. I’m so pleased with this product that I will definitely be looking at other products from 77L.I felt like I paid a LOT for a measuring cup, but it has been well worth it. I am REALLY glad I got it. It is MUCH lighter than the typical Pyrex type (which is important when you have 8 cups hot liquid in it), and though at first it seems fragile, it has held up very well (although, to be fair, I am careful with it - hand wash and dry). At first I was afraid the handle wouldn't support the weight when full and suddenly break during transport, but that has not been a problem. I regularly heat 2Qts of soy milk to near boiling in the microwave (which is above my head - so I have to reach up). I am able to grasp it by the handle only (which is not hot) and do not need to use my other hand to support the very hot beaker part. I used to use a glass mixing bowl for this purpose, but it took two hands and hot pads, it was sloshing above my head, and pouring it out of the bowl always caused spilling. The Pyrex type measuring cups also spilled. But the deep V-shape on the spout of this cup pours nicely every time (it also heats the milk in 1/2 the time for some reason). It occurred to me to see if the entire full measuring cup could fit inside my Instant Pot Duo Plus 6 quart to sterilize it, and it "fits", but you can't seal the lid. Soy milk is high protein, and heating it leaves a thick residue at the liquid surface. This has been easy to wash off with dish soap and water, and after many uses, there does not seem to be any buildup. I am VERY pleased with this purchase and would recommend it.This measuring cup is my favorite!! The glass is not too thick and the handle is big enough my husband can easily hold on to it. It’s lightweight and a perfect asset for baking and cooking.*Amending my review to reflect excellent customer service. I was fully refunded for the broken measuring cup.

I bought this as a replacement for a measuring cup that had lasted for many years. I really liked the sleek look and minimalist design. However, despite being advertised as borosilicate glass, this measuring cup broke after only three months of use. Extremely disapointing.I’m totally satisfied with this 8-cup pitcher!
- It weighs 1 lb 5.5 oz, 40% of the Pyrex one.
- It pours without dribbling over the full duration of volume from full to empty.
- Borosilicate glass is what Pyrex was originally developed from.
- The visual graduations are easy to see!
- The pitcher came double-boxed with foam packing…very safely delivered…clearly would have passed UPS’s drop-test standard.I love this measuring cup. Lightweight and not bulky. Just what I wanted. Durable too. Perfect. It can handle all
temperatures!Mine came in a good condition & well packed. As for me this measuring cup is useful.How many years have I put up with dribbling and spilling out of the old style measuring cup? This one doesn’t and is perfect for every use – measuring and mixing, putting in the microwave, watering indoor plants… Just not big enough to double as a mixing/measure bowl. Sturdy, easy to see markings, excellent.I was looking for a 2 cup measuring cup for liquids that would pour easily, without dribbling down the side of the cup like my other measuring cups. This one is perfect! I also like the lightweight of it. Easy to handle, works great!!
reviews_1s:
I bought this small pitcher to carefully pour water through my small pour-over coffee dripper. I'm making 6oz of coffee, so the 8oz water level is needed. I filled the glass pitcher to the 8oz marking line, heated the water in the microwave, then began to carefully pour the water over my coffee. Dribble dribble dribble right down the front of the glass pitcher. Water on the saucer, water on the place mat, water on the table. FAIL FAIL FAIL. The whole point of this pitcher with the pointed spout is to end messy dripping spills. This pitcher does not. Save your money and save cleaning up your water spilled table. The OXO glass pitcher pours perfectly. Going back to that.I used my OXO 2 cup liquid measuring cup to check the measurements on this cup and it was clearly off (2 cups on my OXO poured to just above the 1 3/4 cup line on this measuring cup). I then checked again using a measuring cup meant for dry ingredients as well as a mason jar with measurements listed, all with the same results.It's a nice cup really, to bad the graduations are not accurate, Seems that a measuring cup should measure accurately. I compared it to my regular measuring cups which are in agreement with a laboratory beaker. This product is Not even close. Pictured is 100 and 300 ML from the lab beaker transferred to this product.I bought this and loved it. Until I cleaned it and I was cleaning it and the bottom broke out. Very disappointed. Thankfully I didn't cut myself!

Can't return it, and I will not buy another one. Remember, be very gentle when you hand wash this measuring cup because the bottom is delicate.The milliliter measurement lines are accurate but the ounces and cups lines are nowhere near accurate. This is the only purpose this item was made for and it provides inaccurate measurements. Total fail.Being a chemistry nerd & doing a lot of bread baking & cooking in general, I needed an accurate heat resistant measuring cup. Only used this perhaps 4-5 times, had milk & some butter being lightly warmed up in microwave and all at once notice fluid coming out the bottom in the microwave tray. I'm thinking, "what the heck?" It had a literal HOLE in the bottom that formed in the microwave with a couple fracture lines going out from the hole. I'VE NEVER SEEN THAT -- and I had a LOT of College level Chemistry classes using actual borosilicate glass with direct gas burners, anyone in College Chem courses knows of what I speak, so for this to do that -- no way is it borosilicate glass. Guess I'll stick with good ol' Pyrex measuring cups. Unfortunate. Not bothering to get a refund, I through away and it's probably been over the 30 day period so I'm outa luck. I would not buy this particular handled beaker - does NOT hold up to even the lightest of use.I really liked it at first. I liked the size and how it stands up slimmer than the other measuring cups. I was careful when choosing this one, because I needed to be sure it was microwave safe. The description said it was, so I bought it. Last weekend, I was baking a cake and needed to melt some butter. I put some into this measuring cup and popped it into the microwave. When I went to remove it, the bottom had a piece of glass about a half inch in diameter broken out, and butter poured out from the bottom.The size of this measuring glass fit an application of mine well. But the glass is very thin and cheap. I've had two of these and the bases cracked within months.Lightly tapped the bottom, once, with a finger and it popped a hole in the bottom.
Very fragile.I don’t even know what happened. All of a sudden liquid started pouring out of the bottom and it had holes in the very thin, weird glass.
